agreement.title: Terms of Service
agreement.section1Content: BusPool is a student-run platform designed to help Durham University students share unused bus tickets. By using our service, you contribute to a more sustainable and cost-effective transportation community.
agreement.section2Content: By accessing and using BusPool, you accept and agree to be bound by these terms. If you disagree with any part, please discontinue use of the service.
agreement.section3Item1: Only share tickets you have personally purchased and used
agreement.section3Item2: Upload clear, readable barcode images that accurately reflect the ticket
agreement.section3Item3: Confirm ticket results honestly and accurately after borrowing
agreement.section3Item4: Do not share expired or previously-used tickets
agreement.section4Item1: You are responsible for maintaining the security of your account credentials
agreement.section4Item2: Reputation scores reflect your reliability and are determined by community feedback
agreement.section4Item3: Accounts with repeated violations may be suspended or permanently banned
agreement.section5Item1: Points have no monetary value and cannot be exchanged for real currency
agreement.section5Item2: The platform reserves the right to adjust point values and earning rules with notice
agreement.section5Item3: Abnormal point transactions may be investigated and reversed
agreement.section6Content: If you have any questions about these terms or need clarification, please reach out to us.
agreement.contactHelp: Help Centre: Available in the app under Profile > Help Centre
agreement.acknowledgment: By continuing to use BusPool, you acknowledge that you understand and agree to these terms.

reputation.title: Reputation System
reputation.howItWorksDesc: Your reputation score (0-100) reflects your reliability as a ticket sharer. Higher reputation unlocks more features and builds trust within the community.
reputation.tip1: Only upload tickets you've personally used today
reputation.tip2: Make sure barcode photos are clear and not expired
reputation.tip3: Don't upload the same ticket multiple times

help.title: Help Centre
help.stillNeedHelpDesc: Can't find what you're looking for? Contact our support team and we'll get back to you as soon as possible.
help.faq_1_q: How do I borrow a ticket?
help.faq_1_a: Browse available tickets on the Home page, select one that fits your route, and tap "Borrow Ticket". The barcode will be revealed after confirmation.
help.faq_2_q: How long can I use a borrowed ticket?
help.faq_2_a: Borrowed tickets are valid until the end of the day (11:59 PM). Each ticket can be borrowed by different users throughout the day.
help.faq_3_q: What if the ticket doesn't work?
help.faq_3_a: If a ticket doesn't work, confirm it as "Invalid" and select the reason. Your points will be refunded. The uploader's reputation may be affected.
help.faq_4_q: How do I upload a ticket?
help.faq_4_a: Tap the Share tab, scan your ticket's barcode using the camera, then upload a photo of the physical ticket as evidence. The ticket must be a Day Ticket you've used today.
help.faq_5_q: How many tickets can I upload per day?
help.faq_5_a: You can upload up to 3 tickets per day. Your reputation must be at least 30 to upload.
help.faq_6_q: What types of tickets are supported?
help.faq_6_a: Currently we support paper Dayrider and DaySaver tickets from Durham bus services. App tickets and digital passes are not supported.
help.faq_7_a: Your reputation (0-100) reflects how reliable you are. Uploading valid tickets increases it, while uploading invalid or already-scanned tickets decreases it.
help.faq_8_q: What happens if my reputation drops below 30?
help.faq_8_a: You will be restricted from uploading new tickets until your reputation recovers. You can regain reputation through successful appeals.
help.faq_9_q: What happens to my reputation if I report a ticket as not working?
help.faq_9_a: Reporting an invalid ticket does not affect your reputation. Only the uploader's reputation may be impacted based on the outcome.
help.faq_10_a: You earn 2 points when someone uses your uploaded ticket successfully. New users receive 10 welcome points upon registration.
help.faq_11_q: How many points does it cost to borrow?
help.faq_11_a: Borrowing a ticket costs 5 points. Points are only deducted after you confirm the ticket worked.
help.faq_12_q: Can I buy points with real money?
help.faq_12_a: No, points cannot be purchased. They are earned through sharing tickets and positive community participation.
help.faq_13_a: Yes. We only share your nickname and reputation score with other users. Your email and personal details are never visible to other users.
help.faq_14_q: How do I report a problem?
help.faq_14_a: You can file an appeal from the Appeals section if you believe a penalty was unfair. For other issues, contact our support team.
help.faq_15_a: Yes, you can delete your account from Settings > Danger Zone. This permanently removes all your data.
